title: Hieracium subgaudinii
uses: Hieracium subgaudinii is a lesser-known species of hawkweed, and its medicinal uses have not been extensively documented. However, some research suggests that the plant may have anti-inflammatory properties due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones. These compounds have been found in various species of the Hieracium genus and may contribute to the plant's potential medicinal applications. Further studies are needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of Hieracium subgaudinii for medicinal use. Traditional folk remedies may also utilize the plant for its supposed healing properties, but these uses are not scientifically verified.
taxonomy: Hieracium subgaudinii is a species of plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. It is classified in the family Asteraceae. Hieracium subgaudinii belongs to the subtribe Cichoriinae and tribe Cichorieae. The species is further classified as a member of the genus Hieracium, which is closely related to the genus Pilosella. This classification helps to group Hieracium subgaudinii with other similar plants within the plant kingdom.

intro: Hieracium subgaudinii, also known as Hawkweed, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and digestive problems, thanks to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium subgaudinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Hawkweed is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
